steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Jack sarcastically says Bartley would carry a leaky pail on his head instead of a hat, just to have something to complain about. This reveals Jack's view of Bartley's character — not anything about weather or hay.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Bartley is worried that Jack is unaware of the coming rain.: Jack's line is about Bartley's tendency to complain, not Bartley's concern that Jack is unaware of the rain. ✗
  • Choice B — Bartley will be able to help Jack with cutting the hay.: nothing in the exchange suggests cooperative intent from Bartley. ✗
  • Choice C — Bartley is a poor judge of the weather.: the pail-on-head image is about inventing reasons to complain, not about weather accuracy. ✗
  • Choice D — Bartley will find fault with any situation.: the sarcasm directly implies Bartley would manufacture a complaint even where none exists — perpetually fault-finding. ✓
That is the correct answer.
